enrichment.short_description: Free time and attendance tracking with GPS, face recognition, and unlimited users, plus premium plans.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Jibble offers free time tracking with GPS, face recognition, and unlimited users, plus premium plans for growing teams.

features.title: Real-time timer and timesheets
features.description: Employees clock in and out with a real-time timer, and hours automatically populate timesheets for review.

features.title: Face recognition verification
features.description: Selfie and face recognition checks confirm the person clocking in is who they claim to be, reducing buddy punching.

features.title: GPS tracking and geofencing
features.description: Location tracking and geofences restrict clock-ins to approved work sites.

features.title: Offline clock-in
features.description: Team members can clock in and out without an internet connection; entries sync once back online.

features.title: PTO and approval workflows
features.description: Time-off requests and timesheet approvals are managed through built-in workflows.

features.title: Invoicing from tracked hours
features.description: Tracked time can be converted directly into client invoices.

features.title: Integrations
features.description: Connects with Slack, Microsoft Teams, QuickBooks, Xero, and Deel.

pros: Free forever plan supports unlimited users
Multiple verification methods (face recognition, GPS, NFC, PIN) reduce time theft
Native iOS, Android, web, and desktop apps plus a Chrome extension
Built-in integrations with Slack, Microsoft Teams, QuickBooks, Xero, and Deel
cons: Premium/Enterprise pricing is not published and requires contacting sales
Advanced features beyond the free tier are not detailed on the public site
Company founding details and headquarters are not disclosed

faqs.question: Is Jibble free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, Jibble offers a free-forever plan with unlimited users, mobile and web clock-in, basic timesheets, and integrations with Slack, Microsoft Teams, and QuickBooks.

faqs.question: Does Jibble support biometric clock-in?
faqs.answer: Yes, Jibble supports face recognition and selfie verification as part of its clock-in options.

faqs.question: Can employees clock in without internet access?
faqs.answer: Yes, Jibble supports offline clock-in, syncing entries once connectivity is restored.

faqs.question: What platforms does Jibble run on?
faqs.answer: Jibble is available on iOS, Android, web browsers, and desktop, plus a Chrome extension and shared kiosk mode.

use_cases.title: Distributed team attendance
use_cases.description: Track clock-ins across multiple locations using GPS and geofencing.
use_cases.audience: Businesses with field or multi-site staff

use_cases.title: Payroll-ready timesheets
use_cases.description: Convert approved timesheets into invoices or export for payroll processing.

features: Slack, Microsoft Teams, and QuickBooks integrations

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Jibble?
paragraphs: Jibble is a time tracking and attendance platform that records employee work hours through mobile, web, and desktop clock-ins.
It combines a real-time timer with verification tools such as face recognition, GPS geofencing, NFC, and PIN codes, and automates timesheets, approvals, and PTO tracking.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Jibble is for
paragraphs: Jibble suits businesses of any size that need attendance verification across distributed or field teams, and want a no-cost starting point before considering premium plans.

enrichment.short_description: Web-based employee time and attendance tracking with a free tier and a $39/month paid plan.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: OpenTimeClock offers free and paid ($39/mo) employee time tracking with GPS, biometric clock-in, and 80+ payroll reports.

features.title: Multiple clock-in methods
features.description: Employees can clock in via username/password, facial recognition, PIN codes, QR codes, or RFID/NFC cards.

features.title: GPS and geofencing
features.description: Location verification and geofencing help prevent buddy punching.

features.title: 80+ predefined reports
features.description: Includes more than 80 PDF and Excel reports for payroll and attendance analysis.

features.title: Overtime and PTO automation
features.description: Automates overtime calculations and PTO accrual tracking.

features.title: Live dashboard
features.description: Real-time notifications and a live dashboard show who is currently clocked in.

features.title: Offline mode
features.description: Supports clock-ins without an active internet connection.

pros: Free plan includes unlimited users and free support
Long-operating company, founded in 1997
Wide range of clock-in and verification methods (biometric, RFID/NFC, QR, GPS)
80+ built-in payroll and attendance reports on paid plans
cons: Free plan limits PDF/Excel report access to 30 days
Full reporting and features require the $39/month paid plan
Headquarters and company size are not disclosed

faqs.question: Is OpenTimeClock free?
faqs.answer: Yes, OpenTimeClock has a free plan with unlimited users, though PDF/Excel report access is limited to the first 30 days.

faqs.question: How much does the paid plan cost?
faqs.answer: The paid plan is $39/month or $399/year, with multi-year prepay options available.

faqs.question: Does OpenTimeClock offer a nonprofit plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, qualifying government agencies, schools, hospitals, and nonprofits can apply for a free full-featured NPO plan.

faqs.question: What clock-in methods are supported?
faqs.answer: OpenTimeClock supports username/password, facial recognition, PIN, QR code, RFID/NFC card, and GPS-based clock-ins.

use_cases.title: Multi-location attendance tracking
use_cases.description: Use geofencing and GPS to verify employee clock-ins at approved locations.

use_cases.title: Payroll reporting
use_cases.description: Generate payroll-ready reports from tracked hours using the 80+ built-in report templates.

features: Full feature access for qualifying nonprofits, schools, hospitals, and government agencies
Paid features unlocked in exchange for a text link to OpenTimeClock on your homepage

long_form_blocks.heading: What is OpenTimeClock?
paragraphs: OpenTimeClock is a web-based employee time tracking and attendance platform, in operation since 1997, that records work hours, manages schedules, and supports payroll processing.
It supports multiple clock-in methods including facial recognition, PIN, QR code, and RFID/NFC cards, and provides more than 80 predefined PDF and Excel reports.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who OpenTimeClock is for
paragraphs: OpenTimeClock fits businesses of any size, plus nonprofits, schools, hospitals, and government agencies that qualify for its free NPO plan.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source, server-side signing software for code, documents, and timestamps, with a paid Enterprise edition.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SignServer is open-source signing software for code, documents, and timestamps, with a free Community edition and paid Enterprise tier.

features.title: Centralized key management
features.description: Signing operations run server-side so cryptographic keys stay in one secured location instead of on individual machines.

features.title: Multi-format code signing
features.description: Supports signing in formats including JAR, CMS, OpenPGP, and Debian packages.

features.title: Timestamping services
features.description: Provides timestamping to prove when a document or artifact was signed.

features.title: Secure boot and OTA signing
features.description: Supports signing for secure boot images and over-the-air firmware updates.

features.title: Post-quantum cryptography support
features.description: Includes support for post-quantum cryptographic algorithms.

features.title: Container and DevOps integration
features.description: Deployable via Docker, Kubernetes Helm charts, and Ansible for CI/CD signing workflows.

pros: Community Edition is free and licensed under LGPL v2.1
Supports a wide range of signing formats and use cases (code, documents, timestamps, firmware)
Enterprise edition adds SLAs and SaaS/Cloud/Appliance deployment options
Backed by Keyfactor, with Docker, Kubernetes, and Ansible deployment support
cons: Keyfactor states the Community Edition is not intended for production signing environments
Enterprise pricing is not publicly listed and requires a trial or sales contact
Requires technical setup (Docker/Kubernetes) rather than a hosted turnkey product

faqs.question: Is SignServer free?
faqs.answer: The Community Edition is free and open source under LGPL v2.1. A paid Enterprise Edition with SLAs and additional security features is also available.

faqs.question: Can I use SignServer Community in production?
faqs.answer: Keyfactor states the Community Edition is intended for learning, testing, and prototyping, not production signing environments; production use is recommended on the Enterprise Edition.

faqs.question: What can SignServer sign?
faqs.answer: SignServer can sign code (JAR, CMS, OpenPGP, Debian packages), documents, timestamps, and secure boot/OTA firmware images.

faqs.question: How is SignServer deployed?
faqs.answer: It can be deployed via Docker, Kubernetes Helm charts, Ansible, or on cloud platforms like AWS and Azure.

use_cases.title: Code signing pipelines
use_cases.description: Integrate centralized signing into CI/CD pipelines for JAR, Debian, or other code artifacts.

use_cases.title: Firmware and secure boot signing
use_cases.description: Sign OTA updates and secure boot images with centrally managed keys.

pricing_plans.price: Contact sales (30-day trial available on AWS/Azure)

long_form_blocks.heading: What is SignServer?
paragraphs: SignServer is open-source signing software, sponsored by Keyfactor, that lets organizations digitally sign documents, code, and timestamps while keeping cryptographic keys centralized and secured server-side.
It ships as a free Community Edition (LGPL v2.1) and a paid Enterprise Edition that adds SLAs, additional security features, and SaaS/Cloud/Appliance deployment options.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who SignServer is for
paragraphs: SignServer is aimed at development and security teams that need centralized code, document, or firmware signing, with the Enterprise Edition intended for production-grade signing environments.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source, AGPL-licensed e-signature app for Nextcloud that lets organizations self-host document signing.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: LibreSign is an open-source AGPL e-signature app for Nextcloud with self-hosted signing plus paid Business and API plans.

features.title: Self-hosted e-signatures
features.description: Runs as a Nextcloud app so organizations sign, manage, and validate documents on infrastructure they control.

features.title: Hybrid signature options
features.description: Supports signing with personal certificates or system-generated certificates.

features.title: QR code validation
features.description: Signed documents can be validated via QR code.

features.title: Batch signing via envelopes
features.description: Groups multiple documents into envelopes for batch signing.

features.title: Certificate revocation and DocMDP
features.description: Implements Certificate Revocation List (CRL) checks and document modification detection (DocMDP).

features.title: Multi-factor authentication
features.description: Adds MFA on top of encrypted signing workflows.

pros: Fully open source under AGPL-3.0, auditable and self-hostable
Integrates directly with Nextcloud, with over 186,000 downloads via the Nextcloud App Store
Supports hybrid (personal or system) certificates and CRL-based revocation checks
Active development led by the LibreCode cooperative since 2020
cons: Public pricing for Business and API Integration plans is not yet available
Requires a Nextcloud environment to run
Commercial plan details require contacting sales

faqs.question: Is LibreSign free?
faqs.answer: Yes, LibreSign's core signing app is free and open source under AGPL-3.0, self-hosted as a Nextcloud app. Paid Business and API Integration plans are also offered, though pricing is not yet public.

faqs.question: What license is LibreSign released under?
faqs.answer: LibreSign is released under the AGPL-3.0 license.

faqs.question: Does LibreSign require Nextcloud?
faqs.answer: Yes, LibreSign is distributed as a Nextcloud app and installed through the Nextcloud App Store.

faqs.question: Who maintains LibreSign?
faqs.answer: LibreSign is developed by the Brazilian cooperative LibreCode (Somos.Coop) since 2020.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted contract signing
use_cases.description: Sign and validate contracts on infrastructure the organization controls, without a third-party SaaS.

use_cases.title: Nextcloud-based document workflows
use_cases.description: Add e-signature validation directly into an existing Nextcloud deployment.

features: API access for integrating with existing systems and workflows

long_form_blocks.heading: What is LibreSign?
paragraphs: LibreSign is an open-source electronic signature app for Nextcloud, developed by the LibreCode cooperative since 2020, that lets organizations sign, manage, and validate documents on self-hosted infrastructure.
It supports hybrid signature methods, QR code-based validation, batch signing via envelopes, and certificate revocation checks, and is released under the AGPL-3.0 license.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who LibreSign is for
paragraphs: LibreSign suits Nextcloud users and organizations that need e-signature capability without handing document data to a third-party SaaS provider.

enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source document management system that scans, OCRs, indexes, and archives paperwork for self-hosting.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Paperless-ngx is a free, open-source, self-hosted document management system with OCR and full-text search.

features.title: OCR ingestion
features.description: Scanned documents are automatically processed with optical character recognition.

features.title: Full-text search
features.description: Search across the text content of all archived documents.

features.title: Tagging and organization
features.description: Documents can be tagged and organized for retrieval.

features.title: Docker Compose deployment
features.description: Deploys via Docker containers, with a pre-configured Docker Compose setup as the easiest path.

features.title: Web-based interface
features.description: An Angular-based web UI provides access to the archive.

pros: Completely free and open source under GPL-3.0
Large, active GitHub community (43.2k stars, 2.9k forks, 149 releases as of the latest check)
Straightforward Docker Compose deployment
Strong OCR and full-text search capabilities
cons: Documents are stored unencrypted; the project warns against running it on an untrusted host
Self-hosting requires ongoing technical maintenance
No official managed/hosted cloud version

faqs.question: Is Paperless-ngx free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Paperless-ngx is fully free and open source under the GPL-3.0 license.

faqs.question: How do I deploy Paperless-ngx?
faqs.answer: The recommended method is Docker Compose, using the project's pre-configured environment.

faqs.question: Is Paperless-ngx safe for sensitive documents?
faqs.answer: The developers advise against running it on an untrusted host since documents are stored unencrypted; local, backed-up deployments are recommended for sensitive records.

faqs.question: What technology powers Paperless-ngx?
faqs.answer: The backend is built in Python (Django) and the frontend uses TypeScript with Angular.

use_cases.title: Home and small-office paperwork archiving
use_cases.description: Scan and OCR physical mail, receipts, and paperwork into a searchable archive.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted document retention
use_cases.description: Run a private, self-hosted archive instead of relying on third-party cloud storage.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Paperless-ngx?
paragraphs: Paperless-ngx is a community-supported, open-source document management system that scans, indexes, and archives physical paperwork.
It runs OCR on incoming documents, supports full-text search and tagging, and is typically deployed with Docker Compose. It is licensed under GPL-3.0 and has over 43,000 GitHub stars.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Paperless-ngx is for
paragraphs: Paperless-ngx suits individuals, families, and small offices who want to digitize paperwork and self-host a searchable document archive rather than use a third-party SaaS.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source document management system with OCR, full-text search, and a REST API, licensed under Apache-2.0.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Papermerge is a free, open-source Apache-2.0 document management system with OCR, full-text search, and a REST API.

features.title: OCR with text overlay
features.description: Scanned documents are processed with OCR and a searchable text layer is overlaid on the original.

features.title: Full-text search
features.description: Search across OCR'd document content.

features.title: Document versioning
features.description: Tracks versions of documents as they are updated.

features.title: Hierarchical folders and tags
features.description: Organizes documents in folders with tags and custom metadata fields.

features.title: Multi-user access
features.description: Supports multiple users with group ownership and document sharing.

features.title: OpenAPI REST API
features.description: Provides an OpenAPI-compliant REST API for integrations.

pros: Permissive Apache-2.0 open-source license
Simple Docker deployment with a single-command basic setup
REST API available for custom integrations
Supports common scan formats: PDF, JPEG, PNG, and TIFF
cons: Smaller GitHub community (508 stars, 109 forks) than some competing open-source DMS projects
No officially documented paid or hosted-cloud tier
Self-hosting requires managing your own database and media storage

faqs.question: Is Papermerge free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Papermerge is free and open source under the Apache-2.0 license.

faqs.question: What file formats does Papermerge support?
faqs.answer: It supports PDF, JPEG, PNG, and TIFF documents, with OCR text overlay.

faqs.question: Does Papermerge have an API?
faqs.answer: Yes, it provides an OpenAPI-compliant REST API.

faqs.question: How is Papermerge deployed?
faqs.answer: It can be deployed via Docker, with a basic single-command setup or more detailed Docker Compose configurations.

use_cases.title: Scanned document archiving
use_cases.description: OCR and index scanned PDFs and images into a searchable, self-hosted archive.

use_cases.title: API-driven document workflows
use_cases.description: Build custom integrations against Papermerge's REST API.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Papermerge?
paragraphs: Papermerge is an open-source document management system built for scanned documents, performing OCR, indexing, and enabling full-text search.
It offers a web UI resembling a desktop file browser, document versioning, tagging, and an OpenAPI-compliant REST API, and is licensed under Apache-2.0.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Papermerge is for
paragraphs: Papermerge fits individuals and small teams who want a self-hosted, API-accessible document archive without licensing costs.

enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source document management system with OCR, workflows, and digital signature verification.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Mayan EDMS is a free, open-source document management system with OCR, workflow automation, and digital signature verification.

features.title: Document versioning
features.description: Stores multiple versions of a document with the ability to download or revert to a previous version.

features.title: Full-text search
features.description: Searches documents by text content, metadata, or other file attributes.

features.title: Workflow engine
features.description: Tracks document state changes through a configurable workflow engine with change history.

features.title: Digital signature verification
features.description: Verifies embedded cryptographic signatures to check document authenticity.

features.title: OCR and auto-categorization
features.description: Extracts text via OCR and supports automatic document categorization.

features.title: Non-destructive image transforms
features.description: Rotate, crop, and flip scanned images without altering the original file.

pros: Free and open source, in active development since 2010
Broad feature set including workflows, OCR, and digital signature verification
Docker-based deployment supported
Commercial support option available alongside the free software
cons: Self-hosting requires technical setup and ongoing maintenance
Official documentation site was inaccessible during review, making evaluation harder without direct GitLab/GitHub access
No public information on company size or headquarters

faqs.question: Is Mayan EDMS free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Mayan EDMS is free and open source; commercial support is also offered separately.

faqs.question: What features does Mayan EDMS include?
faqs.answer: It includes document versioning, full-text search, a workflow engine, OCR, digital signature verification, and non-destructive image transformations.

faqs.question: How is Mayan EDMS deployed?
faqs.answer: It is built on Python/Django and supports Docker-based deployment.

faqs.question: How long has Mayan EDMS been developed?
faqs.answer: The project was introduced in 2010 and has been in continuous active development since.

use_cases.title: Workflow-driven document management
use_cases.description: Route documents through configurable workflows and track their state changes.

use_cases.title: Signature-verified archiving
use_cases.description: Archive documents while verifying embedded digital signatures for authenticity.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Mayan EDMS?
paragraphs: Mayan EDMS is a free, open-source document management system built on Python and Django, in active development since 2010.
It provides document versioning, full-text search, a workflow engine, OCR, digital signature verification, and non-destructive image transformations, with Docker-based deployment support.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Mayan EDMS is for
paragraphs: Mayan EDMS suits organizations that want a self-hosted, workflow-capable document management system without licensing fees, with the option to add commercial support.

enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source personal document organizer with OCR and machine-learning tag suggestions, self-hosted via Docker.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Docspell is a free, open-source AGPLv3 document organizer for homes and small groups, with OCR and ML-based tag suggestions.

features.title: Metadata management
features.description: Organizes documents with tags, correspondents, and custom fields.

features.title: ML-powered suggestions
features.description: Uses machine learning to suggest correspondents and tags for incoming documents.

features.title: OCR processing
features.description: Extracts text from scanned documents and email attachments.

features.title: Full-text search
features.description: Searches across OCR'd document content.

features.title: Email integration
features.description: Ingests documents directly from email.

features.title: REST/HTTP API
features.description: Exposes a REST/HTTP API alongside its web and Android interfaces.

pros: Free and open source under AGPLv3
Machine-learning tag/correspondent suggestions reduce manual sorting
Multiple install methods: Docker, Debian package, Nix, and Helm charts for Kubernetes
Includes an Android app in addition to the web interface
cons: Explicitly targeted at home/household or small-group use rather than large enterprises
Requires self-hosting and technical setup
Backend stack (Scala/Elm) may be less familiar for typical system administrators

faqs.question: Is Docspell free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Docspell is free and open source under the AGPLv3-or-later license.

faqs.question: Who is Docspell designed for?
faqs.answer: Docspell is targeted for home use — families, households, and also smaller groups or companies.

faqs.question: Does Docspell use machine learning?
faqs.answer: Yes, it includes machine-learning-powered suggestions for correspondents and tags.

faqs.question: How is Docspell installed?
faqs.answer: It can be installed via Docker (quickest setup), Debian packages, zip download, Nix, or Helm charts for Kubernetes.

use_cases.title: Household paperwork organization
use_cases.description: Scan, OCR, and auto-tag household mail and receipts.

use_cases.title: Small-group document intake
use_cases.description: Ingest documents from scanners and email into a shared, searchable organizer.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Docspell?
paragraphs: Docspell is a free, open-source personal document organizer that helps individuals and small groups manage paperwork from scanners and email.
It uses OCR and machine-learning-based suggestions for tags and correspondents, and is self-hosted with multiple install options including Docker, Debian, Nix, and Helm.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Docspell is for
paragraphs: Docspell is built for home use — families, households, and small groups or companies — rather than large enterprise deployments.

enrichment.short_description: Lightweight, self-hosted open-source document management system with OCR, encryption, and 2FA.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Teedy is a free, open-source GPL-2.0 document management system with OCR, AES encryption, 2FA, and a REST API.

features.title: OCR and full-text search
features.description: Performs OCR and full-text search across images, PDFs, and Office documents.

features.title: LDAP authentication
features.description: Supports LDAP for centralized user authentication.

features.title: 256-bit AES encryption
features.description: Encrypts stored files with 256-bit AES.

features.title: File versioning
features.description: Tracks file versions as documents change.

features.title: Two-factor authentication
features.description: Adds 2FA on top of standard login.

features.title: REST API and webhooks
features.description: Provides a RESTful API and webhooks for integrations, plus an Android client.

pros: Free and open source under GPL-2.0
Lightweight alternative to larger commercial document management platforms
Strong built-in security: AES encryption, 2FA, and LDAP authentication
Android client available alongside the web app
cons: Default admin credentials (admin/admin) must be changed manually after install
Embedded H2 database is for testing only; PostgreSQL is required for production
Smaller ecosystem than larger open-source DMS competitors

faqs.question: Is Teedy free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Teedy is free and open source under the GPL-2.0 license.

faqs.question: Was Teedy previously known by another name?
faqs.answer: Yes, Teedy was formerly known as Sismics Docs.

faqs.question: How is Teedy deployed?
faqs.answer: The recommended method is Docker, listening on port 8080; manual installation requires Java 11, Tesseract 4, ffmpeg, and mediainfo.

faqs.question: Does Teedy support two-factor authentication?
faqs.answer: Yes, Teedy includes 2FA support alongside LDAP authentication and 256-bit AES encryption.

use_cases.title: Secure document archiving
use_cases.description: Store and search documents with AES encryption, 2FA, and version history.
use_cases.audience: Small businesses and individuals needing security

use_cases.title: Self-hosted DMS alternative
use_cases.description: Replace larger commercial document management platforms with a lightweight, self-hosted option.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Teedy?
paragraphs: Teedy, formerly known as Sismics Docs, is a lightweight, self-hosted open-source document management system released under GPL-2.0.
It provides OCR, full-text search across multiple file formats, 256-bit AES encryption, file versioning, LDAP authentication, 2FA, webhooks, and a REST API, plus an Android client.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Teedy is for
paragraphs: Teedy suits individuals and businesses that want the core features of larger commercial document management systems in a free, self-hosted, lightweight package.

enrichment.short_description: Document management platform with a free open-source Community Edition and paid Business/Enterprise editions.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: LogicalDOC is a document management platform with a free open-source Community Edition and paid Business/Enterprise editions.

features.title: Workflow engine
features.description: Routes documents through configurable circulation and approval workflows.

features.title: Electronic forms and e-signature tools
features.description: Includes tools for electronic forms and document signing.

features.title: Version control and co-authoring
features.description: Tracks document versions and supports collaborative editing.

features.title: Advanced search and retrieval
features.description: Provides advanced search functions across the document repository.

features.title: Microsoft Office integration
features.description: Integrates with Microsoft Office products for document editing.

features.title: Cross-platform and flexible deployment
features.description: Runs on Windows, Linux, and macOS, with cloud, on-premise, and hybrid deployment options.

features.title: NAS integration
features.description: Integrates with QNAP and Synology NAS devices.

pros: Free, open-source Community Edition available alongside paid editions
Flexible deployment: cloud, on-premise, or hybrid
Global office presence across Italy, the US, Germany, Spain, France, and Portugal
Includes a mobile app and NAS integrations
cons: Business and Enterprise edition pricing is not publicly listed and requires a sales quote
Advanced workflow, forms, and e-signature capabilities are reserved for paid editions
Company size is not publicly disclosed

faqs.question: Is LogicalDOC free?
faqs.answer: The Community Edition is free and open source. Business and Enterprise editions are paid, with pricing available only by contacting sales.

faqs.question: Where is LogicalDOC based?
faqs.answer: LogicalDOC is headquartered in Carpi, Italy, with additional offices in the United States, Germany, Spain, France, and Portugal.

faqs.question: When was LogicalDOC founded?
faqs.answer: LogicalDOC's copyright notices date back to 2008.

faqs.question: What deployment options does LogicalDOC support?
faqs.answer: It supports cloud, on-premise, and hybrid cloud deployment, and runs on Windows, Linux, and macOS.

use_cases.title: Enterprise document workflows
use_cases.description: Route documents through approval workflows with version control and co-authoring.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted document management
use_cases.description: Deploy the open-source Community Edition on-premise for full control.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is LogicalDOC?
paragraphs: LogicalDOC is a document management system, headquartered in Carpi, Italy, that helps organizations organize, store, and collaborate on digital documents.
It is offered as a free, open-source Community Edition and a paid, modular Enterprise Edition, with cloud, on-premise, and hybrid deployment options across Windows, Linux, and macOS.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who LogicalDOC is for
paragraphs: LogicalDOC suits organizations that want to start with a free, self-hosted Community Edition and later scale up to a paid Enterprise Edition with workflow, forms, and e-signature features.
